title: Generated Help, Structured Results, Output Channels, and Accessibility · Compiler Case: Pokedex Ts Output Contract Tests
summary: Help and dispatch drift apart, handlers print directly, object iteration changes display order, errors use color alone, and progress rewrites one terminal line that assistive technology cannot follow. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts output contract tests increment in accessible deterministic terminal view layer. Learners…
objectives: Test ordering, empty and unknown fields, Unicode, control characters, no-color mode, narrow width, and stdout or stderr routing.
Prefer append-only restrained status, plain labels, stable headings, no color-only meaning, and narrow-width readability.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
